2026-05-28.text:
One day, I stayed up late at night, I couldn't sleep, I was thinking about it for a while. How robot, artificial intelligence can really replace human beings, and how people can really become "undead" with robots. With this huge idea, I went to sign up for the AP psychology course in my high school, and self learned a lot of machine learning related topics by reading blogs, finding AI foundational essays such as "Attention is All You Need", and breaking through every difficulty I have met. I came to America with a relatively low English score, but no matter how hard the psychological words are, I can always remember it.

As I have learned about psychology and machine learning, I have some ideas about this project. The only difference between all human beings is what they have experienced, what they have learned, since people all have the same brain, but different experiences. Imagine a person who borned in a hood, gun fights happening everyday and the people around him are always teaching him how to be violent and guarded. He will become a criminal eventually since no one really tells him how to be kind and supportive to society. Another person is raised by a wealthy family and people are loving each other, taking care of each other, teaching the kid how to be polite, kind, and literate. He will not be a criminal because he doesn't know how. Even though those 2 people are initially the same. The hardest part must be to mimic the experiences that humans have experienced, and extract the facts and emotions to train AI models to have the exact same memory as the human. Therefore, I initiated this project with a data collection system, and named it as "My Portrait", which means the system can build a user portrait by using the collected data. I made 3 collection modules for this project: screen capture, audio capture, writing capture. It runs on the user's computer silently and builds the portrait with the large language model pipelines.

2026-05-29.text:
Why I'm Building a Copy of Myself
A Personal Essay on the Reasoning Behind "My Portrait"
Author: Zhuoyi Zhang
Written: May 2026
Occasion: Personal reflective writing on my macOS software project "My Portrait" (github.com/joyzhang14-14/My-Portrait).

One day, I stayed up late at night, I couldn't sleep, I was thinking about it for a while. How robot, artificial intelligence can really replace human beings, and how people can really become "undead" with robots. With this huge idea, I went to sign up for the AP psychology course in my high school, and self learned a lot of machine learning related topics by reading blogs, finding AI foundational essays such as "Attention is All You Need", and breaking through every difficulty I have met. I came to America with a relatively low English score, but no matter how hard the psychological words are, I can always remember it.

As I have learned about psychology and machine learning, I have some ideas about this project. I have figured that the only difference between all human beings is what they have experienced, what they have learned, since people all have the same brain, but different experiences. Imagine a person who borned in a hood, gun fights happening everyday and the people around him are always teaching him how to be violent and guarded. He will become a criminal eventually since no one really tells him how to be kind and supportive to society; Another person is raised by a wealthy family and people are loving each other, taking care of each other, teaching the kid how to be polite, kind, and literate. He will not be a criminal because he doesn't know how. Even though those 2 people are initially the same.

One of my friends in elementary school is called Haichen. Back then, we had a lot of great ideas, creative minds. I would say we are literally the same kind of person. I chose to come to America and he chose to stay in China to prepare for Gaokao and Zhongkao (the middle school final exam of China). As I went back to China and asked him about the project and topics we have discussed, he felt numb and lost in thought. He said his parents are putting so much pressure on him for a high score and he has studied until 2am every night. I was shocked, just because of the highly stressed study, and repeated work, he eventually lost his spirit and no longer has a creative mind.

With those experiences and thinking through this topic, I plan to create a project called "My Portrait", it collects what you have seen, your sounds, your typing, and your experience, and eventually become a copy of you. Since people are the sum of what they have experienced, this system can use the data collected to eventually generate a copy of the person. I am 18 right now, with this system to collect my data for decades. When I turn to 88, the system can still use the data to run properly, it can...

I was writing "Natural Monopoly Analysis" not only because it was an assigned essay by my teacher, but also because I am really interested in the economic logic behind an energy company. The hardest part was writing the essay in a structured and persuasive way that proves my idea. I was surprised by the numbers while I was doing the research about this company, 7.7 billion in spending. I am also really distressed by the result after the research, Duke Energy's price is actually lower than others', which means I can't just say Duke Energy's price is too high. After that, I turned to other aspects: why did the price rise? Should the residents pay for the rising price?

I was writing "Why I'm Building a Copy of Myself" because I never write any creative writing except school, exam-required writing. Taking the writing sample 2 as a great opportunity, I initiated my first "creative writing." It is a coding project I was recently working on; I hope it can also be my first entrepreneurial project.
While I saw Haichen lose our vibration of creativity, I felt really sad and disappointed. I can't believe experiences can change a person in this way.
The first draft of this essay is basically full of tech vocabulary, and I handed it to my friend, and even he found it hard to understand. Therefore, I rewrote it. This time, I focused on "the importance of experience." This article also expresses the reason why I made this project. I hope that after people read through it, they will understand my intention, not just full of tech stacks and algorithms.
